records.ety: [L. controversia, fr. controversus turned against, disputed; contro- = contra + versus, p. p. of vertere to turn. See Verse.]

defs: Contention; dispute; debate; discussion; agitation of contrary opinions.
quotes: This left no room for controversy about the title.
A dispute is commonly oral, and a controversy in writing.

defs: Quarrel; strife; cause of variance; difference.
quotes: The Lord hath a controversy with the nations.

defs: A suit in law or equity; a question of right.
quotes: When any man that had a controversy came to the king for judgment.
